saved.phase1Body: Saving reports will be available in a future update.

analysis.shortDescription: A small decorative object with visible hand-finished details and collector appeal.

period.label: Mid-20th century
period.reasoning: The form, surface finish, and visible wear are consistent with post-war studio or small-batch production.

condition.obsMulti1: General surface wear appears consistent with age.
condition.obsMulti2: No major structural damage is visible in the supplied photos.
condition.obsSingle: Condition confidence is limited until more angles are supplied.
condition.obsSubmittedDetails: Condition details were added by the user.
condition.obsDamageReported: The user reported visible condition issues that may affect value.
condition.obsNoVisibleDamage: The user reported no visible damage.
condition.obsMakerMarkAdded: A maker mark or signature photo was added.

quality.item1: Check for chips along rims, corners, handles, and feet.
quality.item2: Inspect the base for maker marks, stamps, labels, or hand signatures.
quality.item3: Ask whether any cracks, repairs, or repainting are present.
quality.sizeProvided: Size details have been provided; compare them with similar objects before buying.

missingPhoto.mark: Close-up of maker's mark, stamp, signature, or base
missingPhoto.back: Back or underside photo to inspect construction and wear
missingPhoto.damage: Close-up of any chips, cracks, repairs, or stains

travel.item1: Pack with rigid support and padding around protruding edges.
travel.item2: Keep receipts and seller details for customs questions when travelling.

sellerQuestion.item1: Do you know the maker, studio, or previous owner?
sellerQuestion.item2: Has the piece had any repairs or restoration?
sellerQuestion.verifySignature: Can the seller confirm the signature, stamp, or maker mark?

decision.reasonPortable: The item appears portable and has enough decorative appeal to justify closer inspection.
decision.reasonDkkAssumption: Suggested max price assumes DKK until a seller currency is provided.
decision.reasonWithSellerCurrency: Suggested max price uses the seller currency supplied in context.
decision.reasonNoVisibleDamage: No visible damage was reported, which modestly improves confidence.
decision.reasonPriceWithinMax: The asking price is at or below the suggested max.
decision.reasonMakerMarkAdded: A maker mark photo improves attribution confidence.
decision.riskAttribution: Attribution is provisional without a clear maker mark.
decision.riskCondition: Condition issues can materially change the valuation.
decision.riskReportedDamage: Reported chips, cracks, or repairs lower the valuation.
decision.riskPriceAboveMax: The asking price is above the suggested max.

I generated a report based on the info you provided. Confidence is {{confidence}}.

Add details from the top card, or tell me anything else here, to improve the confidence level.

photoGuide.title: Photo guide
photoGuide.intro: Good photos make the valuation faster and more reliable. Start with clear overall shots, then add close-ups of the details that prove age, maker, material, and condition.
photoGuide.objectPrompt: What kind of object are you inspecting?

general.title: General tips first
general.tip1: Use bright, even light. Avoid harsh shadows, flash glare, and very warm indoor lighting when you can.
general.tip2: Photograph the whole object on a plain background so the outline, proportions, and scale are easy to read.
general.tip3: Add the back, underside, base, label, signature, stamp, or construction details. These often matter more than the front.
general.tip4: Show condition honestly: chips, cracks, stains, repairs, loose parts, fading, and heavy wear should get close-ups.

ceramics.label: Ceramics and porcelain
ceramics.step1: Take a straight front photo, a side profile, and a top view if the shape or rim matters.
ceramics.step2: Photograph the base clearly, including any stamp, impressed mark, signature, or old label.
ceramics.step3: Add close-ups of rims, handles, feet, cracks, chips, crazing, repairs, and glaze wear.

glassware.label: Glassware
glassware.step1: Shoot against a plain light or dark background so color, thickness, and transparency are visible.
glassware.step2: Photograph the base, pontil mark, rim, seams, bubbles, signatures, and any etched marks.
glassware.step3: Add close-ups of chips, scratches, cloudiness, cracks, and wear around the foot or rim.

jewelry.label: Jewelry and watches
jewelry.step1: Capture the full piece from the front and back, plus a photo next to a coin or hand for scale.
jewelry.step2: Photograph hallmarks, maker marks, clasp details, watch faces, case backs, and movement marks if visible.
jewelry.step3: Show missing stones, worn plating, bent settings, scratches, loose clasps, and any repairs.

prints.label: Prints, paintings, and posters
prints.step1: Take one straight-on photo of the whole image, including margins, frame, and visible edges.
prints.step2: Add close-ups of signatures, edition numbers, stamps, labels, paper texture, and the back.
prints.step3: Show tears, foxing, fading, water marks, frame damage, tape, and any restoration.

furniture.label: Small furniture
furniture.step1: Photograph front, side, back, top, and underside so construction and proportions are visible.
furniture.step2: Add joinery, hardware, maker labels, screws, drawer interiors, feet, and upholstery details.
furniture.step3: Show veneer loss, stains, wobble points, repairs, cracks, loose joints, and fabric wear.

textiles.label: Textiles and rugs
textiles.step1: Take a full flat photo plus a corner or edge so pattern, weave, and size are visible.
textiles.step2: Photograph labels, seams, backs, fringe, embroidery, weave structure, and any maker tags.
textiles.step3: Show holes, stains, fading, moth damage, repairs, thinning, odors if known, and worn edges.

lamps.label: Lamps and lighting
lamps.step1: Capture the full lamp or fixture, plus shade, base, switch, cord, plug, and mounting hardware.
lamps.step2: Photograph labels, maker marks, socket details, material joins, and the underside of the base.
lamps.step3: Show dents, cracks, rewiring, missing parts, shade stains, corrosion, and whether it works.

silver.label: Silver and metalware
silver.step1: Photograph the full object, side profile, inside, handles, feet, and any removable parts.
silver.step2: Add close-ups of hallmarks, maker marks, assay marks, stamped numbers, and engraved initials.
silver.step3: Show dents, splits, worn plating, solder repairs, scratches, tarnish, and bent or loose parts.

confirm.title: Start a new analysis?
confirm.message: The current report and chat will be discarded.

start.requirePhotoPrompt: Found something at a flea market or vintage shop? Upload a photo and I'll help you decide if it's worth buying.
start.photoRequiredReminder: Please attach at least one photo before I can generate a report.
start.photoRequiredFollowUp: Got it — now add a photo of the object so I can run the analysis.

card1.title: Overall view
card1.body: Show the whole object on a simple, contrasting background so shape and scale are easy to judge.

card2.title: Back or base
card2.body: Capture the underside or back—stamps, wear, and construction details often hide there.

card3.title: Maker's mark
card3.body: Add a sharp close-up of any signature, stamp, label, or impressed mark.

card4.title: Damage
card4.body: If you see chips, cracks, crazing, or repairs, a close-up keeps the assessment honest.

error.analysisFailed: I could not update the report. Please try again.

decision.modalSubtitle: Record your final call on this item.

followUp.preflightIntro: Before I make the report, a few quick answers will make the result more useful. You can skip anything.
followUp.askSellerPrice: What is the seller asking price?
followUp.askSellerPriceReason: Helps estimate whether the price is fair.
followUp.askSellerCurrency: Which currency is the price in?
followUp.askSellerCurrencyReason: Keeps the suggested max price in the right currency.
followUp.askBuyingCountry: Where are you buying this item?
followUp.askBuyingCountryReason: Affects import rules and authenticity context.
followUp.askHomeCountry: Where would you take it home to?
followUp.askHomeCountryReason: Helps estimate carry-home cost and travel context.
followUp.askPurpose: What would you mainly use it for?
followUp.askPurposeReason: Your goal changes how strict the recommendation should be.
followUp.askMakersMarkPhoto: Can you upload a close-up of the maker’s mark?
followUp.askMakersMarkPhotoReason: A clear mark photo greatly improves attribution accuracy.
followUp.askConditionDetails: Do you see any chips, cracks, crazing, repairs, or stains?
followUp.askConditionDetailsReason: Condition directly affects value and risk.
